compiled_state.title: As a developer, I can answer design questions during onboarding and receive a .claude/design-notes.md
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Absorb design-onboarding's intake interview into the /dso:onboarding conversation and produce .claude/design-notes.md as an output
**Why**: Design questions are part of the same holistic understanding conversation; running a separate skill loses context and forces users to repeat information
**Scope**:
- IN: Design questions from design-onboarding (vision, user archetypes, golden paths, anti-patterns, tech stack, design system, visual language, accessibility target); conditional activation when project has UI component; .claude/design-notes.md generation with same structured template
- OUT: Reference migration of DESIGN_NOTES.md path (S6); non-design onboarding conversation (S2)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the onboarding conversation includes design questions (vision, archetypes, golden paths, visual language, accessibility) when the project has a UI component
  ← Satisfies: "DESIGN_NOTES.md moves to .claude/design-notes.md, produced as a second output"
- When this story is complete, .claude/design-notes.md is produced as an output of the onboarding conversation with the same structured sections as the current DESIGN_NOTES.md template
  ← Satisfies: "produced as a second output of the onboarding conversation when the project has a UI/design component"
- When this story is complete, design questions are skipped when the project has no UI component (CLI-only, library, infrastructure)
  ← Satisfies: "when the project has a UI/design component"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Design questions come from design-onboarding SKILL.md — port the question content, not the rigid wizard structure

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: All implementation tasks closed. Story complete: design questions integrated into onboarding with conditional activation and .claude/design-notes.md output.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
